symptoms.symptom_name: Macroscopic haematuria with heavy blood clots, acutely worse
symptoms.character: dark red urine with large thick clots, painful to pass

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tumour invasion of the collecting system produces visible haematuria; heavy clots reflect brisk bleeding.
Visible haematuria in an older smoker is bladder/urothelial cancer until proven otherwise.
Heavy haematuria can clot and obstruct bladder outflow.

symptoms.symptom_name: Constant dull heavy right flank / lower-back ache

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A growing renal mass stretches the capsule/collecting system producing a dull constant ache — part of the classic triad.
Constant, non-colicky, non-radiating pain is less typical of a passing stone.

symptoms.symptom_name: Significant unintentional weight loss (~12 kg / 3 months) with cachexia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Marked constitutional weight loss and cachexia are systemic/paraneoplastic features of advanced malignancy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Drenching night sweats and unexplained low-grade fever

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Paraneoplastic (B-symptom-like) fever and night sweats are recognised constitutional features of RCC.
Fever could reflect co-existing infection; cannot be excluded without urine culture.
Non-specific but consistent with anaemia and the systemic burden of advanced malignancy.
Tobacco smoking is a major risk factor for both renal cell and urothelial carcinoma.
Smoking is the strongest modifiable risk factor for bladder/urothelial cancer.

symptoms.symptom_name: Firm, palpable, non-tender unilateral right flank/abdominal mass distinct from the liver edge

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A palpable renal mass completes the classic RCC triad and indicates advanced disease — a clinician palpation finding, not obtainable via telehealth.
A new varicocele can reflect a renal/retroperitoneal mass impeding gonadal venous drainage — a clinician examination finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Conjunctival pallor / anaemia (cachectic, chronically ill appearance)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Anaemia of chronic disease / tumour-related blood loss produces pallor — a clinician examination and blood-test finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Multiphase contrast CT abdomen: solid, heterogeneous, contrast-enhancing renal mass

investigation_details.investigation_name: Multiphase (dedicated) contrast CT abdomen/pelvis; CT urogram for the urinary tract
investigation_details.expected_result: A solid, heterogeneous, contrast-enhancing unilateral renal mass consistent with renal cell carcinoma; urinary-tract assessment for a co-existing urothelial lesion
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The renal mass cannot be characterised or staged and a urinary-tract malignancy cannot be excluded.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cross-sectional imaging is required to characterise the mass and plan staging — never available in real time via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Not on anticoagulant or antiplatelet medication (no drug cause for the bleeding)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of a bleeding medication makes drug-induced haematuria unlikely and keeps a urinary-tract source foremost.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bloods: FBC (anaemia or erythrocytosis), U&E/creatinine, corrected calcium (paraneoplastic hypercalcaemia)

investigation_details.expected_result: Anaemia of chronic disease (or paraneoplastic erythrocytosis), renal function, and possible paraneoplastic hypercalcaemia
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Anaemia, renal impairment and paraneoplastic hypercalcaemia would go undetected.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Paraneoplastic haematological and biochemical changes support the diagnosis — hospital/lab tests, not obtainable via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Classic renal cell carcinoma triad plus constitutional features
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Visible haematuria + dull flank ache + palpable flank mass, with unintentional weight loss and night sweats in a heavy smoker, is a high-risk advanced-malignancy constellation requiring urgent in-person assessment and a fast-track cancer pathway.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Visible haematuria red-flag pathway (any urinary-tract malignancy)
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Any visible haematuria in an older smoker mandates a fast-track haematuria-clinic referral (CT urogram + cystoscopy) regardless of other findings.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Multiphase contrast CT abdomen/pelvis (CT urogram) — urgent
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Characterise the renal mass and assess the urinary tract for a co-existing urothelial lesion; the primary staging investigation.
Assess anaemia/erythrocytosis, renal function and paraneoplastic hypercalcaemia in a patient with heavy haematuria and suspected malignancy.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Formal urine microscopy, culture and cytology
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirm haematuria, exclude co-existing infection and screen for malignant urothelial cells.
Visible haematuria with a suspected mass requires specialist assessment and cystoscopy to complete the urinary-tract work-up.

The fever is most likely paraneoplastic and no infection is proven; empirical antibiotics should not be minted over telehealth and would be an error of commission without culture and in-person assessment.
